page: Amazon Web Services m8ib.metal-96xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server instance featuring dedicated Intel processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers 192 physical cores and 384 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) with a base clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1536.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This bare-metal instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. It features a high-capacity network interface with a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfer for distributed environments. The absence of local storage and GPUs represents a design tradeoff, prioritizing raw CPU execution and high-throughput networking over localized disk I/O or hardware-accelerated graphics processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8ib.metal-96xl offers a dense consolidation platform for enterprise workloads. The bare-metal deployment model eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly suitable for large-scale container orchestration, high-performance web serving, enterprise database hosting, and intensive CPU-bound batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.metal-96xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors. It features 192 physical cores, 384 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz, and 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a high-speed 200 Gbps baseline network interface. The bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for resource-intensive enterprise applications. The m8ib.metal-96xl is optimized for large-scale workloads such as enterprise databases, container orchestration, high-throughput web serving, and heavy CPU-bound batch processing.
